attacking_style: Long-range attacks (40km to go) on unexpected terrain, targeting KOM points and selective climbs rather than sustained summit finishes. Opportunistic on transitions and flat-to-descent sections when peloton is passive.
team_tactics: Minimal team support evident; rides independently with focus on KOM jersey strategy rather than stage wins. Selective breakaway participation, targeting specific climbs for points rather than every opportunity.
terrain_preference: Flat-to-descent finales and selective mid-mountain climbs where tactical positioning matters more than pure climbing power. Excels when peloton is disorganized or hesitant. Improved descending ability shown.
vulnerability: Hesitates when required to chase alone against strong opposition. Arrives too late when dragging groups rather than attacking solo. Loses effectiveness on pure sustained climbs against superior climbers.
key_patterns: Targets KOM classification through selective climb strategy rather than every breakaway (2022, 2017)
Long-range solo attacks 40km+ out on unexpected terrain when peloton passive (2017 Giro)
Shows tactical hesitation when needing to chase strong riders alone, waits for group (2018)
Improved descending from earlier career weaknesses, can win on downhill finales (2017)
Positive form building into mountain stages after initially being distanced in GC (2020)
form_trajectory: neutral: Limited recent data (2022 KOM focus) but historically shows tactical intelligence improving with race progression. Adaptation ability demonstrated but consistency uncertain.
